judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `bmi` is associated with a higher `objective_response` probability.
judgments.rationale: BMI-associated higher objective response probability in AML is not a consensus textbook or treatment-guideline biomarker relationship.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `ast_u_l` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.
judgments.rationale: AST level as a continuous predictor of lower objective response in AML is not a standard guideline-level response predictor.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `alt_u_l` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.
judgments.rationale: A quantitative association between higher ALT and lower objective response is a dataset-derived prognostic claim rather than an established AML guideline paradigm.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `total_bilirubin_mg_dl` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.
judgments.rationale: A quantitative association between higher bilirubin and lower objective response is not a standard textbook or guideline biomarker-treatment association in AML.

judgments.text: Patients with `flt3_itd` are more likely to receive `treatment_midostaurin` than patients without `flt3_itd`.
judgments.rationale: Preferential use of midostaurin in FLT3-ITD AML reflects established guideline-directed targeted therapy selection.

judgments.text: Patients with `idh1_mutation` are more likely to receive `treatment_ivosidenib` than patients without `idh1_mutation`.
judgments.rationale: Preferential use of ivosidenib in IDH1-mutated AML is an established biomarker-drug association.

judgments.text: Patients with `idh2_mutation` are more likely to receive `treatment_enasidenib` than patients without `idh2_mutation`.
judgments.rationale: Preferential use of enasidenib in IDH2-mutated AML is an established biomarker-drug association.

judgments.text: Among patients with `flt3_itd`, `treatment_midostaurin` is associated with higher `objective_response` than in FLT3-ITD patients not receiving midostaurin.
judgments.rationale: Improved response with midostaurin among FLT3-ITD patients is consistent with established FLT3-targeted therapy paradigms.

judgments.text: Among patients with `flt3_itd`, `treatment_gilteritinib` is associated with higher `objective_response` than in FLT3-ITD patients not receiving gilteritinib.
judgments.rationale: Improved response with gilteritinib among FLT3-mutated AML patients is a standard targeted-therapy association.

judgments.text: After adjustment for baseline covariates, the `treatment_ivosidenib` by `idh1_mutation` interaction is negatively associated with `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: A negative adjusted interaction between ivosidenib and IDH1 mutation contradicts the established expected predictive relationship and is a subgroup-conditional dataset finding.

judgments.text: After adjustment for baseline covariates, the `treatment_enasidenib` by `idh2_mutation` interaction remains positively associated with `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: A positive enasidenib-by-IDH2 interaction aligns with the established IDH2-targeted treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: After adjustment for baseline covariates, the `treatment_gilteritinib` by `flt3_any` interaction remains positively associated with `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: A positive gilteritinib-by-FLT3 interaction aligns with the established FLT3-targeted treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: The association of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` with `objective_response` is smaller in patients aged 75 years or older than in younger patients.
judgments.rationale: This age-stratified attenuation of venetoclax-azacitidine objective response in patients aged 75 or older is a subgroup-conditional effectiveness claim rather than a standard guideline-level oncology association.

judgments.text: Patients with `npm1_mutation` = 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `npm1_mutation` = 0.
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutated AML is an established favorable-risk molecular subtype often associated with better treatment response.

judgments.text: Patients with poor performance status (`ecog_ps` >= 2) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `ecog_ps` < 2.
judgments.rationale: Poor ECOG performance status is a standard adverse clinical factor for treatment tolerance and response in oncology and AML.

judgments.text: Patients with high leukemic burden (`wbc_k_per_ul` >= 50) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `wbc_k_per_ul` < 50.
judgments.rationale: High leukocyte count reflects leukemic burden and is a well-established adverse prognostic feature in AML.

judgments.text: Patients with high marrow blast burden (`blast_pct_marrow` >= 70) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `blast_pct_marrow` < 70.
judgments.rationale: High marrow blast burden is a conventional marker of greater disease burden and poorer response risk in AML.

judgments.text: Patients with low albumin (`albumin_g_dl` < 3.5) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `albumin_g_dl` >= 3.5.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a specific predictor of AML objective response is a supportive-care or frailty-associated hypothesis rather than a guideline-level disease paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients with clinically meaningful weight loss (`weight_loss_pct_6mo` >= 10) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with less weight loss.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss predicting AML objective response is a cachexia/frailty hypothesis not typically treated as an AML guideline biomarker.

judgments.text: Patients with elevated inflammation (`crp_mg_l` >= 10) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `crp_mg_l` < 10.
judgments.rationale: CRP-defined inflammation predicting AML objective response is not a standard guideline or textbook response determinant.

judgments.text: Patients with adverse genetics (`tp53_mutation` = 1) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `tp53_mutation` = 0.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation is a canonical adverse-risk AML genetic feature associated with poor response and outcomes.

judgments.text: Patients with `complex_karyotype` = 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `complex_karyotype` = 0.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a canonical adverse-risk cytogenetic feature in AML associated with poor response and outcomes.

judgments.text: Patients with `unfit_for_intensive` = 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `unfit_for_intensive` = 0, potentially reflecting therapy selection.
judgments.rationale: A higher response rate among patients unfit for intensive therapy attributed to therapy selection is a dataset-specific confounding hypothesis rather than established consensus.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` = 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax plus azacitidine improving response in older or intensive-therapy-ineligible AML is an established treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, `npm1_mutation` = 1 remains associated with a higher probability of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutated AML is widely recognized as a favorable-response molecular subgroup, including in lower-intensity venetoclax-based contexts.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, each one-point increase in `ecog_ps` remains associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status as a predictor of poorer treatment response and outcome is a standard oncology principle.

judgments.text: The response advantage of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` is larger in patients with `unfit_for_intensive` = 1 than in patients with `unfit_for_intensive` = 0.
judgments.rationale: A specific interaction claiming a larger venetoclax/azacitidine response advantage by intensive-therapy fitness status goes beyond the usual guideline statement of treatment eligibility.

judgments.text: Within patients with `unfit_for_intensive` = 1, `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` = 1 is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than no venetoclax/azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Benefit of venetoclax/azacitidine in patients unfit for intensive AML therapy is established consensus.

judgments.text: Within patients with `unfit_for_intensive` = 0, `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` = 1 does not show the same positive `objective_response` association and may be slightly lower than no venetoclax/azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: A possible absent or negative association for venetoclax/azacitidine specifically among intensive-therapy-fit patients is a subgroup-conditional claim not established as guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The response advantage of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` is larger in patients with `npm1_mutation` = 1 than in patients with `npm1_mutation` = 0.
judgments.rationale: A differential treatment-effect interaction between venetoclax/azacitidine and NPM1 mutation status is more specific than the established favorable prognosis of NPM1-mutated AML.

judgments.text: Among patients receiving `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` = 1, `npm1_mutation` = 1 is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than `npm1_mutation` = 0.
judgments.rationale: Higher response rates to venetoclax-based therapy in NPM1-mutated AML are already a well-described association.

judgments.text: The response advantage of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` is smaller in patients with `tp53_mutation` = 1 than in patients with `tp53_mutation` = 0.
judgments.rationale: Reduced benefit and adverse outcomes with TP53-mutated AML on venetoclax/azacitidine are well established.

judgments.text: Among patients receiving `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` = 1, `tp53_mutation` = 1 is associated with a lower `objective_response` rate than `tp53_mutation` = 0.
judgments.rationale: Lower response to venetoclax/azacitidine among TP53-mutated AML compared with TP53-wild-type disease is a standard recognized association.

judgments.text: The positive `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` association is especially large in patients jointly characterized by `unfit_for_intensive` = 1 and `npm1_mutation` = 1.
judgments.rationale: Although venetoclax/azacitidine is standard for unfit AML and NPM1-mutated disease is venetoclax-sensitive, the joint subgroup claim of an especially large association is not guideline-level consensus.

judgments.text: The joint presence of `tp53_mutation` = 1 attenuates the `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` association in the `unfit_for_intensive` = 1 subgroup.
judgments.rationale: TP53-mutated AML is known to have adverse outcomes, but a specific interaction attenuating venetoclax/azacitidine benefit within unfit patients is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond standard guideline statements.

judgments.text: In the high-response subgroup `unfit_for_intensive` = 1, `npm1_mutation` = 1, and `tp53_mutation` = 0, `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` = 1 is associated with a much higher `objective_response` rate than no venetoclax/azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: This combines fitness, NPM1 status, TP53 wild-type status, and treatment response into a specific high-response subgroup not typically stated as a treatment-guideline paradigm.

judgments.text: In patients with `flt3_itd` = 1, `treatment_midostaurin` = 1 is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than no midostaurin.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin benefit in FLT3-mutated, including FLT3-ITD, AML is an established targeted-therapy association in standard AML treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: In patients with `flt3_itd` = 1, `treatment_gilteritinib` = 1 is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than no gilteritinib.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib activity in FLT3-mutated AML is an established targeted-therapy association reflected in current AML management.

judgments.text: In patients with `idh1_mutation` = 1, `treatment_ivosidenib` = 1 is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than no ivosidenib.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib benefit in IDH1-mutated AML is a standard targeted-therapy association.

judgments.text: In patients with `idh2_mutation` = 1, `treatment_enasidenib` = 1 is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than no enasidenib.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib benefit in IDH2-mutated AML is a standard targeted-therapy association.

judgments.text: The association of intensive chemotherapy (`treatment_7plus3` = 1) with `objective_response` is lower in patients with `unfit_for_intensive` = 1 than in patients with `unfit_for_intensive` = 0.
judgments.rationale: Lower suitability and expected benefit of intensive induction chemotherapy in patients unfit for intensive therapy is a core AML treatment principle.

judgments.text: The association of intensive chemotherapy (`treatment_7plus3` = 1) with `objective_response` is lower in patients age 75 or older than in younger patients.
judgments.rationale: Reduced benefit and tolerability of intensive chemotherapy in very old AML patients is an established guideline-level consideration.

judgments.text: The favorable association of `npm1_mutation` = 1 with `objective_response` is weaker in patients who also have `flt3_itd` = 1.
judgments.rationale: The adverse modifying effect of FLT3-ITD on otherwise favorable NPM1-mutated AML biology is a well-established AML risk-stratification concept.

judgments.text: The negative association of `tp53_mutation` = 1 with `objective_response` is stronger in patients who also have `complex_karyotype` = 1.
judgments.rationale: The adverse role of TP53 mutation and complex karyotype is established in AML, but a specific interaction in which complex karyotype strengthens TP53-associated nonresponse is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Older age (`age_years` >= 75) is associated with a lower `objective_response` rate than age below 75.
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting lower response or poorer treatment tolerance is a standard AML oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: Female sex (`sex_female` = 1) is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than `sex_female` = 0.
judgments.rationale: Female sex being associated with higher objective response is not a standard guideline-level predictive association for AML therapy.

judgments.text: `secondary_aml` = 1 is associated with a lower adjusted probability of `objective_response` than `secondary_aml` = 0.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML is an established adverse clinical category associated with poorer response and outcomes.

judgments.text: `atrial_fibrillation` = 1 is associated with a lower adjusted probability of `objective_response` than `atrial_fibrillation` = 0.
judgments.rationale: Atrial fibrillation as an independently lower-response subgroup is a comorbidity-specific association not part of established AML treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: Patients with `liver_mets` = 1 have a slightly higher `objective_response` rate, and patients with `her2_amplification` = 1 have a slightly lower `objective_response` rate, than patients without those features; these exploratory non-AML fields are expected to have small effects.

judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib sensitivity in FLT3-TKD-mutated AML is an established part of the FLT3-targeted therapy paradigm.

judgments.text: Among patients with `idh1_mutation`, `treatment_ivosidenib` is associated with a larger increase in `objective_response` than among patients without `idh1_mutation`.
judgments.rationale: IDH1 mutation predicting response to the IDH1 inhibitor ivosidenib is established guideline-level oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Among patients with `idh2_mutation`, `treatment_enasidenib` is associated with a larger increase in `objective_response` than among patients without `idh2_mutation`.
judgments.rationale: IDH2 mutation predicting response to the IDH2 inhibitor enasidenib is established guideline-level oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: The positive association between `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` and `objective_response` is larger among patients with `unfit_for_intensive` than among patients without `unfit_for_intensive`.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax plus azacitidine as a standard regimen for patients unfit for intensive AML therapy is an established treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: The positive association between `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` and `objective_response` is larger among patients with `npm1_mutation` than among patients without `npm1_mutation`.
judgments.rationale: Enhanced venetoclax-azacitidine response specifically in NPM1-mutated AML is a subgroup biomarker claim beyond core guideline treatment selection.

judgments.text: Patients who have both `unfit_for_intensive` and `npm1_mutation` show an especially large positive `objective_response` interaction with `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` beyond the pairwise effects.
judgments.rationale: A three-way interaction among unfitness, NPM1 mutation, and venetoclax-azacitidine response beyond pairwise effects is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Among patients with `unfit_for_intensive`, `tp53_mutation` reduces the positive `objective_response` interaction with `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine`.
judgments.rationale: TP53 attenuation of venetoclax-azacitidine benefit specifically within the unfit subgroup is a conditional interaction claim beyond the general known adverse prognosis of TP53-mutated AML.

judgments.text: Patients with `complex_karyotype` have a smaller positive `objective_response` association with `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` than patients without `complex_karyotype`.
judgments.rationale: Although complex karyotype is an established adverse AML feature, a specific attenuation of venetoclax-azacitidine objective-response benefit by complex karyotype is a treatment-effect interaction beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with `complex_karyotype` have a smaller positive `objective_response` association with intensive `treatment_7plus3` than patients without `complex_karyotype`.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a standard adverse-risk AML feature in the intensive chemotherapy setting, so reduced response benefit with 7+3 aligns with established consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with `npm1_mutation` have a larger positive `objective_response` association with intensive `treatment_7plus3` than patients without `npm1_mutation`.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation is a well-established favorable-risk AML marker, particularly in intensively treated patients when not overridden by adverse features.

judgments.text: The favorable `npm1_mutation` association with `objective_response` is smaller among patients with `tp53_mutation` than among patients without `tp53_mutation`.
judgments.rationale: A TP53-dependent weakening of the favorable NPM1 response association is a specific subgroup interaction rather than a standard guideline-level paradigm.

judgments.text: The favorable `npm1_mutation` association with `objective_response` is smaller among patients with `complex_karyotype` than among patients without `complex_karyotype`.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines already recognize that NPM1-mutated AML with adverse cytogenetics such as complex karyotype should not retain the usual favorable-risk interpretation.

judgments.text: Patients with `secondary_aml` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without `secondary_aml` after adjustment for treatment and clinical covariates.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML is a well-established adverse clinical category associated with poorer treatment response and outcomes.

judgments.text: Older patients, measured by higher `age_years`, have a lower `objective_response` rate, with a negative response-probability effect per 1 SD increase.
judgments.rationale: Older age is a standard adverse clinical prognostic factor in AML and is routinely incorporated into treatment and risk assessment.

judgments.text: Patients with `sex_female` equal to 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `sex_female` equal to 0 after adjustment for treatment and clinical covariates.
judgments.rationale: A female-sex-associated higher adjusted objective-response rate is not a standard AML treatment-guideline or textbook consensus biomarker.

judgments.text: Patients with `stage_iv` equal to 1 have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `stage_iv` equal to 0.
judgments.rationale: Advanced stage is an established adverse prognostic factor for progression-free survival in oncology guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: A 1-SD increase in `ecog_ps` is associated with lower adjusted `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Poorer ECOG performance status is a standard, widely recognized adverse prognostic factor for treatment outcomes and PFS.

judgments.text: Patients with `has_brain_mets` equal to 1 have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients without brain metastases.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases are a well-established marker of advanced disease burden and poorer prognosis.

judgments.text: A 1-SD increase in `age_years` is associated with higher adjusted `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting higher adjusted PFS is not a standard consensus prognostic paradigm and would be context-dependent.

judgments.text: A 1-SD increase in `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with lower adjusted `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Cancer-associated weight loss/cachexia is an established adverse prognostic feature.

judgments.text: A 1-SD increase in `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher adjusted `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin reflecting better nutritional and inflammatory status is a well-known favorable prognostic marker.

judgments.text: A 1-SD increase in `ki67_pct` is associated with lower adjusted `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Higher Ki-67 is an established marker of tumor proliferation and generally worse prognosis in breast oncology contexts.

judgments.text: Patients with `pik3ca_mutation` equal to 1 have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients without `pik3ca_mutation`.
judgments.rationale: A general claim that PIK3CA mutation independently predicts lower PFS is not a standard guideline-level consensus association.

judgments.text: Patients with `er_positive` equal to 1 have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `er_positive` equal to 0.
judgments.rationale: ER positivity is a well-established favorable prognostic and predictive feature in breast cancer.

judgments.text: Patients with `her2_positive` equal to 1 have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `her2_positive` equal to 0.
judgments.rationale: HER2 positivity has historically been an established adverse biologic feature, despite modern targeted therapy changing outcomes.

judgments.text: A 1-SD increase in `ldh_u_l` is associated with lower adjusted `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH is a standard adverse prognostic marker in advanced cancer and lower PFS would not go beyond textbook oncology expectations.

judgments.text: Patients with `treatment_palbociclib` equal to 1 have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients without `treatment_palbociclib`.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib improving PFS in hormone receptor-positive breast cancer is an established treatment-guideline paradigm.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is larger when `er_positive` equals 1 than when `er_positive` equals 0.
judgments.rationale: Greater palbociclib benefit in ER-positive disease follows the established indication for CDK4/6 inhibitors in endocrine-responsive breast cancer.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is smaller when `her2_positive` equals 1 than when `her2_positive` equals 0.
judgments.rationale: A HER2-positive-specific attenuation of palbociclib-associated PFS is a subgroup interaction claim beyond standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is smaller when `pik3ca_mutation` equals 1 than when `pik3ca_mutation` equals 0.
judgments.rationale: Reduced palbociclib benefit specifically in PIK3CA-mutant tumors is a biomarker-interaction claim not established as a standard treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is larger when `pr_positive` equals 1 than when `pr_positive` equals 0.
judgments.rationale: Larger benefit in PR-positive disease is broadly consistent with known endocrine sensitivity in hormone receptor-positive breast cancer.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is larger when `her2_low` equals 1 than when `her2_low` equals 0.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low status modifying palbociclib-associated PFS is not an established guideline-level paradigm.

judgments.text: Among patients with `er_positive` equal to 1, `her2_positive` equal to 0, and `pik3ca_mutation` equal to 0, `treatment_palbociclib` equal to 1 is associated with higher adjusted `pfs_months` than no `treatment_palbociclib`.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER-positive HER2-negative disease is standard, and excluding PIK3CA mutation does not make the core claim novel.

judgments.text: Within patients with `er_positive` equal to 1 and `her2_positive` equal to 0, the positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is smaller when `pik3ca_mutation` equals 1.
judgments.rationale: Within ER-positive HER2-negative disease, PIK3CA mutation weakening palbociclib benefit is a nonstandard subgroup-conditional interaction claim.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_tamoxifen` with `pfs_months` is larger when `er_positive` equals 1 than when `er_positive` equals 0.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit being greater in ER-positive tumors is a foundational established breast oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_trastuzumab` with `pfs_months` is larger when `her2_positive` equals 1 or `her2_amplification` equals 1 than otherwise.
judgments.rationale: HER2 positivity or amplification predicting greater benefit from trastuzumab is an established breast cancer treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_olaparib` with `pfs_months` is larger when either `brca1_mutation` or `brca2_mutation` equals 1 than when both equal 0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1/2 mutation predicting greater benefit from olaparib and other PARP inhibitors is an established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is larger in triple-negative patients (`er_positive=0`, `pr_positive=0`, and `her2_positive=0`) than in other patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in triple-negative breast cancer is an established treatment paradigm, especially in appropriate PD-L1-positive or high-risk settings.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` with `pfs_months` is larger when `has_brain_mets` equals 1 than when `has_brain_mets` equals 0.
judgments.rationale: A preferential progression-free survival benefit from sacituzumab govitecan specifically in patients with brain metastases is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond standard consensus.

judgments.text: Higher `ecog_ps` is associated with lower `pfs_months`; each 1-point increase in `ecog_ps` decreases progression-free survival after adjustment.

judgments.text: Having `stage_iv` disease is associated with lower `pfs_months` than non-stage-IV disease after adjustment.

judgments.text: Patients with `has_brain_mets` have lower `pfs_months` than patients without `has_brain_mets`, after adjustment.

judgments.text: Patients with `bone_mets` have lower `pfs_months` than patients without `bone_mets`, after adjustment.

judgments.text: Patients with `node_positive` disease have lower `pfs_months` than patients without node-positive disease, after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Node-positive disease is a standard adverse prognostic factor and would be covered in guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Each 1 g/dL higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher `pfs_months`, after adjustment.

judgments.text: Each 5 percentage-point higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with lower `pfs_months`, after adjustment.

judgments.text: Each 10 mg/L higher `crp_mg_l` is associated with lower `pfs_months`, after adjustment.

judgments.text: Each 1-unit higher `nlr` is associated with lower `pfs_months`, after adjustment.

judgments.text: Each 10 percentage-point higher `ki67_pct` is associated with lower `pfs_months`, after adjustment.

judgments.text: The positive association between `treatment_tamoxifen` and `pfs_months` is larger in `er_positive` patients than in `er_positive` = 0 patients.

judgments.text: The positive association between `treatment_tamoxifen` and `pfs_months` is larger in `postmenopausal` patients than in premenopausal patients.
judgments.rationale: A stronger tamoxifen-PFS association specifically in postmenopausal versus premenopausal patients is a subgroup interaction claim beyond standard consensus.

judgments.text: The positive association between `treatment_palbociclib` and `pfs_months` is larger in `er_positive` patients than in `er_positive` = 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit is an established standard paradigm in hormone receptor/ER-positive metastatic breast cancer, not ER-negative disease.

judgments.text: The positive association between `treatment_trastuzumab` and `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `her2_amplification` than in patients without `her2_amplification`.

judgments.text: The positive association between `treatment_olaparib` and `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `brca2_mutation` than in patients without `brca2_mutation`.

judgments.text: The positive association between `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` and `pfs_months` is larger in triple-negative patients (`er_positive` = 0, `pr_positive` = 0, `her2_positive` = 0) than in other patients.

judgments.text: The positive association between `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` and `pfs_months` is larger in patients with visceral metastases (`liver_mets`, `adrenal_mets`, `contralateral_lung_mets`, or `pleural_effusion`) than in patients without visceral metastases.

judgments.text: The positive association between `treatment_pembrolizumab` and `pfs_months` is larger in triple-negative patients (`er_positive` = 0, `pr_positive` = 0, `her2_positive` = 0) than in other patients.

judgments.text: The positive association between `treatment_pembrolizumab` and `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `crp_mg_l` >= 10 than in patients with lower `crp_mg_l`.

judgments.text: Patients with `node_positive` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than node-negative patients after adjustment for stage, metastatic disease, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.
judgments.rationale: Node-positive disease is a conventional adverse prognostic factor in breast cancer.

judgments.text: Patients with `er_positive` set to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than ER-negative patients after adjustment for other receptor markers, disease severity, labs, and treatments.

judgments.text: After accounting for `er_positive`, patients with `pr_positive` set to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than PR-negative patients.

judgments.text: Patients with `her2_positive` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than HER2-negative patients after adjustment for hormone receptors, other biomarkers, disease severity, labs, and treatments.

judgments.text: Patients with `her2_low` set to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients without HER2-low status after adjustment for `her2_positive`, hormone receptors, disease severity, labs, and treatments.

judgments.text: Patients with `pik3ca_mutation` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients without `pik3ca_mutation` after adjustment for clinical severity, receptor markers, labs, and treatments.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation is established as an actionable biomarker, but an independent adverse PFS association is not a settled textbook or guideline paradigm.

judgments.text: A 10 percentage-point higher `ki67_pct` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for clinical severity, receptor markers, labs, and treatments.

judgments.text: A 1 g/dL higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease severity, biomarkers, inflammatory markers, and treatments.

judgments.text: A 5 percentage-point higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease severity, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.

judgments.text: A 100 U/L higher `ldh_u_l` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease severity, biomarkers, other labs, and treatments.

judgments.text: After accounting for albumin, LDH, weight loss, stage, biomarkers, and treatments, higher `crp_mg_l` and higher `nlr` remain independently associated with lower mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Higher `tumor_size_cm` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for stage, nodal status, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.
judgments.rationale: Larger primary tumor size is a conventional measure of tumor burden and an established adverse prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_palbociclib` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_palbociclib` after adjustment for clinical severity, biomarkers, labs, and other treatments.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib improving PFS in hormone-receptor-positive advanced breast cancer is an established treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `er_positive` set to 1 than in ER-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: Greater palbociclib benefit in ER-positive disease follows from the established indication and biology of CDK4/6 inhibitors in HR-positive breast cancer.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is smaller in tumors with high proliferation, measured as a 10 percentage-point higher `ki67_pct`.
judgments.rationale: A Ki-67-dependent attenuation of palbociclib benefit is a subgroup interaction claim beyond routine guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `pik3ca_mutation` set to 1 than in patients without `pik3ca_mutation`.
judgments.rationale: Reduced palbociclib benefit specifically in PIK3CA-mutant tumors is a biomarker-treatment interaction claim not established as standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The association of `treatment_tamoxifen` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in patients with `er_positive` set to 1.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit in ER-positive breast cancer is an established endocrine-therapy paradigm and guideline-level association.

judgments.text: The association of `treatment_trastuzumab` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in patients with `her2_positive` set to 1 or `her2_amplification` set to 1.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit being concentrated in HER2-positive or HER2-amplified breast cancer is a canonical guideline-level predictive association.

judgments.text: The association of `treatment_olaparib` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in patients with either `brca1_mutation` or `brca2_mutation` set to 1.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1/2-mutated breast cancer is an established PARP-inhibitor indication and not novel.

judgments.text: The association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in triple-negative patients (`er_positive`=0, `pr_positive`=0, and `her2_positive`=0).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in triple-negative breast cancer is an established immunotherapy treatment paradigm in appropriate clinical settings.

judgments.text: The association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in triple-negative patients (`er_positive`=0, `pr_positive`=0, and `her2_positive`=0).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan activity in triple-negative breast cancer is an established guideline-recognized treatment association.

judgments.rationale: Independent adjusted associations of metastatic-site and common comorbidity indicators with lower PFS are prognostic, multivariable dataset-specific claims rather than standard treatment-guideline paradigms.

judgments.text: Patients with `er_positive` equal to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients with `er_positive` equal to 0.
judgments.rationale: ER-positive breast cancer is an established biologically favorable and endocrine-responsive subtype associated with better outcomes than ER-negative disease.

judgments.text: Patients with `her2_positive` equal to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `her2_positive` equal to 0.
judgments.rationale: HER2-positive breast cancer has long been recognized as a more aggressive subtype, despite major improvement with HER2-targeted therapy.

judgments.text: Patients with `stage_iv` equal to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `stage_iv` equal to 0.

judgments.text: Patients with `has_brain_mets` equal to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `has_brain_mets` equal to 0.

judgments.text: Among patients with `er_positive` equal to 1, those with `treatment_tamoxifen` equal to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than those with `treatment_tamoxifen` equal to 0.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit in ER-positive breast cancer is a foundational and guideline-established endocrine therapy association.

judgments.text: Among patients with `her2_positive` equal to 1, those with `treatment_trastuzumab` equal to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than those with `treatment_trastuzumab` equal to 0.

judgments.text: Among patients with `brca1_mutation` equal to 1 or `brca2_mutation` equal to 1, those with `treatment_olaparib` equal to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than those with `treatment_olaparib` equal to 0.
judgments.rationale: Improved outcomes with olaparib in BRCA1/2-mutated breast cancer reflects an established PARP inhibitor paradigm.

judgments.text: Among patients with both `brca1_mutation` and `brca2_mutation` equal to 0, `treatment_olaparib` has little or no positive association with mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Among patients with `autoimmune_disease` equal to 0, those with `treatment_pembrolizumab` equal to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than those with `treatment_pembrolizumab` equal to 0.

judgments.text: Among patients with `autoimmune_disease` equal to 1, the positive association between `treatment_pembrolizumab` and mean `pfs_months` is attenuated compared with patients without autoimmune disease.
judgments.rationale: Attenuated pembrolizumab benefit specifically in patients with autoimmune disease is a subgroup-modification claim beyond routine guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Among patients with high inflammatory burden, defined as `crp_mg_l` above the 75th percentile or `nlr` above the 75th percentile, `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` equal to 1 is associated with higher mean `pfs_months` than no sacituzumab govitecan.

judgments.rationale: Reduced palbociclib benefit in the highest Ki67 quartile is a predictive subgroup claim not established as guideline consensus.

judgments.rationale: Higher albumin as a favorable prognostic marker is a well-established general oncology association.

judgments.text: After adjustment for disease severity and treatments, a one-standard-deviation increase in `ldh_u_l` is associated with lower `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_trastuzumab` with `pfs_months` is strongest when both `her2_positive` and `her2_amplification` equal 1, producing a positive three-way interaction among `treatment_trastuzumab`, `her2_positive`, and `her2_amplification`.
judgments.rationale: This is a standard HER2-targeted therapy association, since trastuzumab benefit in breast cancer is expected to be greatest in HER2-positive/HER2-amplified tumors.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_tamoxifen` with `pfs_months` is larger when `pr_positive` equals 1, even after accounting for `er_positive` and disease severity.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` with `pfs_months` is smaller among patients with `bone_mets` equal to 1 than among patients with `bone_mets` equal to 0.
judgments.rationale: A bone-metastasis-specific attenuation of sacituzumab govitecan benefit is a subgroup-conditional claim not found as a standard guideline paradigm.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_olaparib` with `pfs_months` is attenuated among patients with `chronic_kidney_disease` equal to 1 after accounting for BRCA mutation status and severity.
judgments.rationale: CKD-related attenuation of olaparib PFS benefit after BRCA adjustment is a comorbidity-specific predictive claim beyond standard BRCA-based PARP inhibitor guidance.

judgments.text: Among patients not fully captured by `her2_positive`, `her2_low` modifies the association between `treatment_trastuzumab` and `pfs_months`, with a positive `treatment_trastuzumab` by `her2_low` interaction after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: A positive trastuzumab interaction in HER2-low disease outside conventional HER2-positive classification is speculative and not standard trastuzumab guidance.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is smaller as `ecog_ps` increases, yielding a negative `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `ecog_ps` interaction.
judgments.rationale: A pembrolizumab-by-ECOG predictive interaction for PFS is more specific than the general known prognostic effect of poor performance status.

judgments.text: The association between `treatment_tamoxifen` and `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `postmenopausal` equal to 1 than in patients with `postmenopausal` equal to 0 after accounting for hormone receptor status and severity.
judgments.rationale: A postmenopausal-specific reduction in tamoxifen association after hormone receptor adjustment is not a standard consensus predictive rule.

judgments.text: `pik3ca_mutation` equal to 1 is associated with shorter `pfs_months`, and this negative association is partly offset among patients with `treatment_palbociclib` equal to 1.

judgments.text: The negative association of `stage_iv` with `pfs_months` is more severe when `liver_mets` equals 1, yielding a negative `stage_iv` by `liver_mets` interaction after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Worse PFS in stage IV disease with visceral liver metastases reflects an established adverse prognostic pattern rather than a novel oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is attenuated among patients with `interstitial_lung_disease_history` equal to 1.
judgments.rationale: Attenuated pembrolizumab benefit in patients with prior interstitial lung disease is a toxicity/comorbidity subgroup hypothesis beyond standard biomarker-based guidance.

judgments.text: Patients with `hemoglobin_g_dl` in the lowest quartile have lower `pfs_months`, and this negative association is not fully rescued by `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan`.
judgments.rationale: While anemia is a known adverse prognostic factor, the claim that low hemoglobin is not fully rescued by sacituzumab govitecan is a treatment-conditional assertion beyond standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Older age, measured per 10-year increase in `age_years`, is associated with shorter `pfs_months`, and the association of `treatment_tamoxifen` with `pfs_months` does not materially increase with age.

judgments.text: `venous_thromboembolism_history` equal to 1 is associated with shorter `pfs_months`, especially among patients with `stage_iv` equal to 1.

judgments.text: Adding biomarker-matched treatment interactions (`treatment_palbociclib` by `er_positive`, `treatment_tamoxifen` by `er_positive`, `treatment_trastuzumab` by `her2_positive`, and `treatment_olaparib` by any BRCA1/2 mutation) improves prediction of `pfs_months` beyond main effects and severity covariates.

judgments.text: Higher `cea_ng_ml` is associated with lower `pfs_months`, with the effect estimated per one standard deviation increase in CEA after adjustment.

judgments.text: Higher `crp_mg_l` is associated with lower `pfs_months`, with the effect estimated per one standard deviation increase in CRP after adjustment.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_encorafenib` have a larger increase in `pfs_months` when `braf_v600e` is present than when `braf_v600e` is absent.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib` have a larger increase in `pfs_months` when `her2_amplified` is present than when `her2_amplified` is absent.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_cetuximab` have a smaller increase in `pfs_months` when `right_sided_primary` is present than when `right_sided_primary` is absent.
judgments.rationale: Reduced benefit from anti-EGFR therapy for right-sided colorectal primary tumors is an established treatment-guideline consideration.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_bevacizumab` have a smaller increase in `pfs_months` when `venous_thromboembolism_history` is present than when `venous_thromboembolism_history` is absent.
judgments.rationale: Prior venous thromboembolism is mainly a safety-risk consideration for bevacizumab rather than an established guideline-level predictor of smaller PFS benefit.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_pembrolizumab` have a smaller increase in `pfs_months` when `autoimmune_disease` is present than when `autoimmune_disease` is absent.
judgments.rationale: Autoimmune disease is a clinical caution and toxicity-risk factor for pembrolizumab, but reduced PFS benefit in that subgroup is not a standard oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: Each 10-year higher `age_years` value is associated with longer `pfs_months` after adjustment for sex, ECOG performance status, stage IV status, sidedness, LDH, albumin, liver metastases, and bone metastases.
judgments.rationale: Older age being independently associated with longer PFS is not an established colorectal oncology guideline or textbook prognostic paradigm.

judgments.text: A one-point higher `ecog_ps` score is associated with shorter `pfs_months` after adjustment for age, sex, stage IV status, sidedness, LDH, albumin, liver metastases, and bone metastases.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status is a standard adverse prognostic factor for progression and survival in oncology.

judgments.text: Patients with `stage_iv` equal to 1 have shorter `pfs_months` than patients with `stage_iv` equal to 0 after adjustment for baseline prognostic factors.

judgments.text: A 1 g/dL higher `albumin_g_dl` value is associated with longer `pfs_months` after adjustment for baseline prognostic factors.

judgments.text: Patients in the top quartile of `cea_ng_ml` (>=10.070 ng/mL) have shorter `pfs_months` than patients below that threshold after adjustment for baseline prognostic factors.

judgments.text: Patients with `bone_mets` equal to 1 have slightly longer `pfs_months` than patients without bone metastases after adjustment for baseline prognostic factors.

judgments.text: Patients with `liver_mets` equal to 1 have shorter `pfs_months` than patients without liver metastases after adjustment for baseline prognostic factors.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_regorafenib` with `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `right_sided_primary` equal to 1 than in left-sided patients.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_regorafenib` with `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `kras_mutation` equal to 1 than in KRAS-wildtype patients.

judgments.text: The negative association of `kras_mutation` with `pfs_months` is attenuated in patients with `right_sided_primary` equal to 1 compared with left-sided patients.

judgments.text: Patients with `pericardial_effusion` equal to 1 have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `pericardial_effusion` equal to 0.

judgments.text: Patients with `contralateral_lung_mets` equal to 1 have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `contralateral_lung_mets` equal to 0.

judgments.text: Each 1 g/dL increase in `hemoglobin_g_dl` is associated with higher adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 100 K/uL increase in `platelets_k_ul` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 1 K/uL increase in `alc_k_ul` is associated with higher adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 100 U/L increase in `alkaline_phosphatase_u_l` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 50 U/L increase in `ast_u_l` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Worse liver enzyme elevation is a broadly established adverse prognostic marker in oncology rather than a novel disease-specific paradigm.

judgments.text: Each 1 mg/dL increase in `total_bilirubin_mg_dl` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 1 mg/dL increase in `creatinine_mg_dl` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 10 mg/dL increase in `bun_mg_dl` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 1 mEq/L increase in `sodium_meq_l` is associated with higher adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 1 kg/m2 increase in `bmi` is associated with higher adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 100 mg/dL increase in `glucose_mg_dl` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Hyperglycemia and metabolic dysfunction are established nonspecific adverse prognostic factors in cancer populations.

judgments.text: Each 1 percentage-point increase in `spo2_pct` is associated with higher adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: The positive association between `albumin_g_dl` and `pfs_months` is smaller as `weight_loss_pct_6mo` increases.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is smaller among patients with `autoimmune_disease` equal to 1.
judgments.rationale: Reduced pembrolizumab benefit specifically among patients with autoimmune disease is a subgroup-conditional immunotherapy efficacy claim beyond standard consensus guidance.

judgments.text: In a final treatment-biomarker interaction model, `treatment_sotorasib` with `kras_g12c` equal to 1 is the dominant positive treatment interaction for `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant tumors is an established targeted-therapy biomarker association in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: In the same final model, the `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `pdl1_tps` interaction is positive for `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS as a positive predictive biomarker for pembrolizumab benefit is a standard immunotherapy paradigm.

judgments.text: In the same final model, the `treatment_olaparib` by `brca2_mutation` interaction is positive for `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation predicting benefit from the PARP inhibitor olaparib is an established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: In the same final model, the `treatment_osimertinib` by `egfr_mutation` interaction is positive for `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation predicting benefit from osimertinib is a canonical targeted-therapy association in oncology.

judgments.text: Each 10-year higher `age_years` is associated with longer `pfs_months` after adjustment for treatment, stage, histology, smoking, ECOG performance status, metastatic burden, inflammatory/nutritional labs, and key biomarkers.
judgments.rationale: A claim that older age independently predicts longer PFS is counter to typical oncology prognostic expectations and is not a standard guideline-level paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients with `stage_iv` equal to 1 have shorter `pfs_months` than patients with `stage_iv` equal to 0 after multivariable adjustment.

judgments.text: Patients with `ecog_ps` equal to 2 have shorter `pfs_months` than patients with `ecog_ps` equal to 0 after multivariable adjustment.

judgments.text: Patients with `has_brain_mets` equal to 1 have shorter `pfs_months` than patients without brain metastases after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases are widely recognized as an adverse prognostic feature associated with shorter PFS.

judgments.text: Patients with `histology` equal to squamous have shorter `pfs_months` than patients with adenocarcinoma histology after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Histologic subtype differences in prognosis and treatment outcomes, including worse outcomes for squamous versus adenocarcinoma in many lung cancer contexts, are established clinical knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with `smoking_status` equal to current have shorter `pfs_months` than patients with `smoking_status` equal to never after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Current smoking is a known adverse prognostic factor and is commonly associated with poorer cancer outcomes.

judgments.text: Patients with `sex_female` equal to 1 have shorter `pfs_months` than patients with `sex_female` equal to 0 after multivariable adjustment.

judgments.text: Each 1 percentage-point higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with shorter `pfs_months` after multivariable adjustment.

judgments.text: Each 1 g/dL higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with longer `pfs_months` after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin as a marker of better nutritional and inflammatory status is an established favorable prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Each 100 U/L higher `ldh_u_l` is associated with shorter `pfs_months` after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH is a widely recognized adverse prognostic biomarker reflecting tumor burden or aggressive disease biology.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_sotorasib` have longer `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_sotorasib` after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit as a KRAS G12C-targeted therapy in advanced NSCLC is an established treatment paradigm, even if phrased as an adjusted association.

judgments.text: The association between `treatment_sotorasib` and longer `pfs_months` is concentrated in patients with `kras_g12c`; the `treatment_sotorasib` by `kras_g12c` interaction is positive.

judgments.text: Among patients with `kras_g12c` equal to 1, `treatment_sotorasib` is associated with longer `pfs_months` than no sotorasib.
judgments.rationale: Benefit of sotorasib among KRAS G12C-positive patients is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Among patients with `kras_g12c` equal to 0, `treatment_sotorasib` is not associated with longer `pfs_months` than no sotorasib.
judgments.rationale: Lack of expected sotorasib benefit without KRAS G12C is implicit in the established target-dependent indication.

judgments.text: The association between `treatment_sotorasib` and `pfs_months` differs for patients with `egfr_mutation`; the `treatment_sotorasib` by `egfr_mutation` interaction is negative.
judgments.rationale: An EGFR-mutant subgroup-specific negative interaction with sotorasib is a cross-biomarker claim beyond standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The association between `treatment_sotorasib` and `pfs_months` differs for patients with `alk_fusion`; the `treatment_sotorasib` by `alk_fusion` interaction is negative.
judgments.rationale: An ALK fusion subgroup-specific negative interaction with sotorasib is not a standard guideline or textbook association.

judgments.text: The association between `treatment_sotorasib` and `pfs_months` differs for patients with `brca2_mutation`; the `treatment_sotorasib` by `brca2_mutation` interaction is negative.

judgments.text: The association between `treatment_sotorasib` and `pfs_months` differs for patients with `tmb_high`; the `treatment_sotorasib` by `tmb_high` interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: A positive interaction between TMB-high status and sotorasib benefit is not part of established sotorasib biomarker guidance.

judgments.text: Patients with `stk11_mutation` equal to 1 have shorter `pfs_months` than patients without `stk11_mutation` after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation as an adverse prognostic marker associated with shorter outcomes in NSCLC is broadly established.

judgments.text: Patients with `brca2_mutation` equal to 1 have shorter `pfs_months` than patients without `brca2_mutation` after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation as an independent marker of shorter PFS in this setting is not a standard consensus oncology association.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_pembrolizumab` have longer `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_pembrolizumab` after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS is an established oncology treatment paradigm in guideline-supported immunotherapy settings.

judgments.text: Higher `pdl1_tps` is associated with longer `pfs_months`, with each 0.50 higher value of `pdl1_tps` increasing PFS after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS as a favorable or predictive biomarker for immune checkpoint inhibitor benefit is a standard established association.

judgments.text: The association between `treatment_pembrolizumab` and longer `pfs_months` is stronger when `pdl1_tps` is higher; the `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `pdl1_tps` interaction per 0.50 higher `pdl1_tps` is positive.

judgments.text: The association between `treatment_osimertinib` and longer `pfs_months` is stronger in patients with `egfr_mutation`; the `treatment_osimertinib` by `egfr_mutation` interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit being stronger in EGFR-mutant disease is a canonical guideline-level targeted therapy association.

judgments.text: The association between `treatment_olaparib` and longer `pfs_months` is stronger in patients with `brca2_mutation`; the `treatment_olaparib` by `brca2_mutation` interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit being stronger in BRCA2-mutant tumors reflects the established PARP inhibitor homologous recombination deficiency paradigm.

judgments.text: Higher inflammatory and tumor-burden markers `ldh_u_l`, `crp_mg_l`, `nlr`, and `weight_loss_pct_6mo` are associated with shorter mean `pfs_months`, while higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with longer mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for baseline clinical factors.
judgments.rationale: These inflammatory, nutritional, and tumor-burden markers are established adverse prognostic factors for PFS rather than a new oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients with `stage_iv`, `has_brain_mets`, `liver_mets`, or `bone_mets` have shorter mean `pfs_months` than patients without those metastatic burden features after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Advanced stage and metastatic burden at brain, liver, or bone sites are standard adverse prognostic features in oncology guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Patients with `egfr_mutation` have longer `pfs_months` specifically when treated with `treatment_osimertinib`; the `treatment_osimertinib` by `egfr_mutation` interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: EGFR-mutant NSCLC benefit from osimertinib is an established guideline-level predictive association.

judgments.text: Patients with `kras_g12c` have longer `pfs_months` specifically when treated with `treatment_sotorasib`; the `treatment_sotorasib` by `kras_g12c` interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C predicting benefit from sotorasib is an established targeted-therapy paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients with `brca2_mutation` have longer `pfs_months` specifically when treated with `treatment_olaparib`; the `treatment_olaparib` by `brca2_mutation` interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-specific olaparib benefit is guideline-established in some cancers but is not a standard consensus association for an NSCLC-like dataset.

judgments.text: Patients with `tmb_high` derive more `pfs_months` benefit from `treatment_pembrolizumab` than patients without `tmb_high`; the interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: High tumor mutational burden predicting greater immune checkpoint inhibitor benefit is a broadly established immunotherapy biomarker concept.

judgments.text: Higher continuous `pdl1_tps` increases the `pfs_months` benefit associated with `treatment_pembrolizumab`; the `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `pdl1_tps` interaction per 50 TPS points is positive.
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS predicting greater pembrolizumab benefit is a standard guideline-level immunotherapy association.

judgments.text: Patients with `stk11_mutation` derive less `pfs_months` benefit from `treatment_pembrolizumab`; the `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `stk11_mutation` interaction is negative.

judgments.text: `egfr_mutation` reduces the `pfs_months` benefit associated with `treatment_pembrolizumab`; the `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `egfr_mutation` interaction is negative.
judgments.rationale: Reduced checkpoint inhibitor benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is a recognized consensus pattern reflected in treatment sequencing recommendations.

judgments.text: `kras_g12c` reduces the `pfs_months` benefit associated with `treatment_pembrolizumab`; the `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `kras_g12c` interaction is negative.
judgments.rationale: A KRAS G12C-specific reduction in pembrolizumab benefit is a subgroup-conditional claim that is not established as guideline consensus.

judgments.text: `liver_mets` attenuates the `pfs_months` benefit associated with `treatment_pembrolizumab`; the `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `liver_mets` interaction is negative.

judgments.text: `has_brain_mets` reduces the `pfs_months` benefit associated with `treatment_osimertinib`; the `treatment_osimertinib` by `has_brain_mets` interaction is negative.
judgments.rationale: A negative brain-metastasis interaction with osimertinib benefit is not a standard paradigm, especially given osimertinib's established CNS activity.

judgments.text: `alk_fusion`, `ros1_fusion`, `ret_fusion`, and `met_exon14_skipping` each have positive adjusted associations with `pfs_months`, reflecting actionable oncogene subgroups with longer disease control.
judgments.rationale: Longer disease control in actionable oncogene subgroups such as ALK, ROS1, RET, and MET exon 14 is established oncology consensus.

judgments.text: `pleural_effusion`, `pericardial_effusion`, `adrenal_mets`, and `contralateral_lung_mets` are associated with shorter adjusted mean `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Effusions and metastatic disease burden sites are conventional adverse prognostic features in advanced lung cancer.

judgments.text: `hemoglobin_g_dl` and `spo2_pct` are positively associated with `pfs_months`, while `alkaline_phosphatase_u_l` and `cea_ng_ml` are negatively associated with `pfs_months` after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Hemoglobin, oxygenation, alkaline phosphatase, and CEA reflect well-known clinical prognostic and disease-burden associations.

judgments.text: Baseline comorbidity variables `chronic_kidney_disease`, `heart_failure`, `copd`, and `autoimmune_disease` are associated with shorter adjusted mean `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Baseline comorbidity burden being associated with worse outcomes is a standard clinical prognostic concept.

judgments.text: Apparent main effects of `treatment_pembrolizumab`, `treatment_sotorasib`, `treatment_olaparib`, and `treatment_osimertinib` on `pfs_months` are positive after baseline adjustment, before accounting for predictive biomarker interactions.
judgments.rationale: Positive adjusted treatment main effects before biomarker-interaction modeling are dataset- and model-specific claims rather than guideline consensus.

judgments.text: After jointly including key treatment-biomarker interactions, only `treatment_sotorasib` by `kras_g12c` remains a large positive predictor of longer `pfs_months`, while `treatment_osimertinib` by `egfr_mutation`, `treatment_olaparib` by `brca2_mutation`, and `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `tmb_high` are near zero.
judgments.rationale: The claim that only the sotorasib-KRAS G12C interaction remains large while canonical EGFR, BRCA2, and TMB interactions are near zero contradicts or goes beyond established paradigms.

judgments.text: `stk11_mutation` has at most a small negative modification of `treatment_pembrolizumab` benefit after accounting for `tmb_high` and other treatment-biomarker interactions.
judgments.rationale: The magnitude of STK11-mediated attenuation of pembrolizumab benefit after accounting for TMB and other interactions is a nuanced subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: The three-way `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `tmb_high` by `stk11_mutation` interaction is slightly negative but close to zero, indicating no clear TMB-high-specific STK11 attenuation.
judgments.rationale: A near-zero three-way pembrolizumab-TMB-high-STK11 interaction is a specific higher-order interaction claim not found in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: `histology` modifies `treatment_pembrolizumab` benefit, with squamous histology having a different `pfs_months` treatment association than non-squamous histology.
judgments.rationale: Histology-based differences in NSCLC treatment effects and pembrolizumab subgroup outcomes are part of established clinical framing rather than a novel oncology claim.

judgments.text: `smoking_status` modifies `treatment_pembrolizumab` benefit, with current smokers having a different `pfs_months` treatment association than never/former smokers.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status is recognized as correlated with immunotherapy responsiveness, but a specific current-smoker interaction modifying pembrolizumab PFS benefit is not a guideline-level treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: A scan of binary covariates for `treatment_pembrolizumab` will show only small interaction effects on `pfs_months`, with the largest absolute interaction under 0.10 months.
judgments.rationale: A dataset-wide claim that all binary covariate interactions with pembrolizumab are uniformly tiny is a pipeline-specific empirical pattern, not an established textbook association.

judgments.text: A scan of binary covariates for `treatment_osimertinib` will not identify a dominant positive interaction with `pfs_months`; even the largest interactions will be small in magnitude.
judgments.rationale: A negative scan result asserting no dominant osimertinib interaction is a cohort-specific interaction claim beyond standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The strongest clinically coherent patterns in this cohort are a positive `treatment_sotorasib` by `kras_g12c` interaction, positive `albumin_g_dl`, negative `stage_iv`, negative `has_brain_mets`, and negative `weight_loss_pct_6mo`; most other hypothesized treatment-biomarker interactions are near zero.
judgments.rationale: The listed coherent signals are dominated by established associations, including sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C disease and standard adverse prognostic factors such as stage IV, brain metastases, weight loss, and low albumin.

judgments.text: For every 10-year increase in `age_years`, adjusted mean `pfs_months` is higher.
judgments.rationale: An adjusted association in which older age predicts higher PFS is not an established consensus prognostic paradigm for advanced NSCLC.

judgments.text: For each 1-point increase in `ecog_ps`, adjusted mean `pfs_months` is lower.

judgments.text: Patients with `stage_iv` equal to 1 have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `stage_iv` equal to 0.

judgments.text: The joint presence of `stage_iv` equal to 1 and `has_brain_mets` equal to 1 lowers `pfs_months` more than the sum of their separate adjusted effects.
judgments.rationale: A supra-additive interaction between stage IV disease and brain metastases is a subgroup-conditional effect beyond standard guideline-level prognosis.

judgments.text: Patients with `histology` equal to `squamous` have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `histology` equal to `adenocarcinoma`.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is larger for each 10 percentage-point increase in `pdl1_tps`.

judgments.text: Each 1 mg/dL increase in `creatinine_mg_dl` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 10 mg/dL increase in `bun_mg_dl` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 1 mEq/L increase in `sodium_meq_l` is associated with higher adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 1 kg/m2 increase in `bmi` is associated with higher adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 100 mg/dL increase in `glucose_mg_dl` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: Each 1 percentage-point increase in `spo2_pct` is associated with higher adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is smaller among patients with `autoimmune_disease` equal to 1.

judgments.text: In a final treatment-biomarker interaction model, `treatment_sotorasib` with `kras_g12c` equal to 1 is the dominant positive treatment interaction for `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: In the same final model, the `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `pdl1_tps` interaction is positive for `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: In the same final model, the `treatment_olaparib` by `brca2_mutation` interaction is positive for `pfs_months`.

judgments.text: For each additional year in `age_years`, adjusted mean `pfs_months` is higher.

judgments.text: Patients with `sex_female=1` have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `sex_female=0`.

judgments.text: Patients with `smoking_status=current` have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `smoking_status=never`.

judgments.text: Patients with `smoking_status=former` have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `smoking_status=never`.

judgments.text: The response effect from `treatment_docetaxel` is smaller among patients with `mcrpc` than among patients without `mcrpc`.
judgments.rationale: A smaller docetaxel response effect in mCRPC than in non-mCRPC disease is a conditional comparative-effect claim not established as guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Each one-level increase in `ecog_ps` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status is a standard adverse prognostic factor associated with poorer treatment response and outcomes across oncology.

judgments.text: Patients with `visceral_mets` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients without `visceral_mets`, independent of treatment and disease severity.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases are an established adverse prognostic feature in advanced prostate cancer and are associated with poorer outcomes.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in log-transformed `psa_ng_ml` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.
judgments.rationale: Higher PSA is an established adverse disease-burden/prognostic marker in prostate cancer and would not be considered a novel response association.

judgments.text: Each one-point increase in `gleason_score` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.
judgments.rationale: Higher Gleason score reflects more aggressive prostate cancer and is a standard adverse prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `albumin_g_dl` is associated with a higher probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in log-transformed `ldh_u_l` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in log-transformed `crp_mg_l` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `nlr` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `hemoglobin_g_dl` is associated with a higher probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Patients with `liver_mets` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients without `liver_mets`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Each one-point increase in `gleason_score` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.
judgments.rationale: Higher Gleason score reflects more aggressive prostate cancer and is a standard adverse prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `albumin_g_dl` is associated with a higher probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin generally reflects better nutritional/performance status and is a well-known favorable prognostic association in oncology.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in log-transformed `ldh_u_l` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia is a standard marker of poor clinical condition and worse cancer outcomes.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in log-transformed `crp_mg_l` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `nlr` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `hemoglobin_g_dl` is associated with a higher probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.
judgments.rationale: Higher hemoglobin and absence of anemia are established favorable clinical prognostic features in advanced cancer.

judgments.text: Patients with `liver_mets` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients without `liver_mets`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Patients with `bone_mets` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients without `bone_mets`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Patients with `pleural_effusion` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients without `pleural_effusion`, independent of treatment and disease severity.
judgments.rationale: Pleural effusion is a well-established adverse clinical/prognostic feature in advanced oncology and would not be considered a novel response-associated factor.

judgments.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in log-transformed `alkaline_phosphatase_u_l` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, independent of treatment and disease severity.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase is a standard marker of liver or bone disease burden and poor prognosis, so an association with lower response is not novel.

judgments.text: Patients with `chronic_kidney_disease` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients without `chronic_kidney_disease`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Patients with `heart_failure` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients without `heart_failure`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Patients with `diabetes_mellitus` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients without `diabetes_mellitus`, independent of treatment and disease severity.

judgments.text: Patients with `autoimmune_disease` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients without `autoimmune_disease`, independent of treatment and disease severity.
